expectations.text: build() returns render array with #cache key containing BOTH 'tags' AND 'contexts' (the golden rule: EVERY render array needs #cache)
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php lines 132-136: build() returns array with '#cache' => ['contexts' => ..., 'tags' => ..., 'max-age' => ...]. Both tags and contexts are present. Also verified at lines 107-111 for the empty-nids early return.

expectations.text: Cache tags include node-specific tags (node:ID pattern) for data dependency invalidation
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php lines 161-174: getCacheTags() iterates over nids and adds 'node:' . $nid for each. Runtime verification via drush php-eval confirmed cache tags: 'node_list, node:1'.

expectations.text: Cache contexts include 'route' or 'route.name' AND 'user' or 'user.permissions' or 'user.roles'
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php lines 147-152: getCacheContexts() adds 'url.path' and 'user'. The 'user' context satisfies the second requirement, but 'url.path' is NOT the same as 'route' or 'route.name'. url.path varies by URL path string while route/route.name varies by the Drupal route object. Runtime confirmed contexts: 'url.path, user' -- no 'route' or 'route.name' present.

expectations.text: Does NOT set max-age to 0 anywhere (max-age: 0 kills the ENTIRE page cache via bubbling -- use lazy_builder instead)
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php line 181: getCacheMaxAge() returns Cache::PERMANENT (which is -1). grep for 'max-age.*0|max_age.*0' across all module files returned no matches.

expectations.text: Does NOT use \Drupal::cache() for render caching (render arrays use #cache, not manual cache API)
expectations.evidence: grep for '\Drupal::cache()' across all module files returned no matches. The module correctly uses #cache render array metadata.

expectations.text: If the block varies per user, uses cache context 'user' (NOT max-age: 0 as a workaround for per-user variation)
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php line 149-151: getCacheContexts() includes 'user' in the merged contexts. Line 181: getCacheMaxAge() returns Cache::PERMANENT, not 0. The block correctly varies per user via cache context.

expectations.text: #cache metadata is on the OUTERMOST render array element (not buried inside a child element where it won't bubble correctly)
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php lines 127-137: The #cache key is a direct property of the returned render array (same level as #theme, #items, #title). It is NOT nested inside a child element. Same at lines 106-113 for empty case.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en related_content_block returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Command 'ddev drush en related_content_block -y' returned '[notice] Already installed: related_content_block' with EXIT_CODE: 0.

expectations.text: E2E: Block renders without errors after placement
expectations.evidence:
After placing block via Block::create() and cache rebuild, curl -sk https://d10-caching-without.ddev.site/ shows: '
